commit.message:
Fix dynamic Function new.target leak and generator callable dispatch

- Add push_dynamic_function_call_new_target() helper to isolate
  new.target for BUILTIN_CTOR_FUNCTION/BUILTIN_NEW_FUNCTION calls,
  preventing outer constructor new.target from leaking into
  CreateDynamicFunction and corrupting the prototype chain.

- Fix __fn_body__ callable_expr to use function*/async function/
  async function* for generator/async dynamic functions.

- Split eval+call for generators: eval the function expression first,
  then call it separately so the generator's bytecode outlives the
  eval scope (fixes generators returning {done:true} immediately).

- Route all dynamic generator calls in runner.rs through
  vm_call_function_value instead of manual yield parsing, fixing
  return value handling in dynamic generators.

Cluster 32: 581→595 pass (+14), Cluster 29: 701→704 pass (+3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip secondary vtable emission on COFF path to fix LNK2005 duplicate RTTI symbols

Secondary vtables (with non-zero offset_to_top) are an Itanium/ELF concept.
On the COFF/MSVC path, emitting them causes duplicate RTTI symbols like
??_R1.?AVDerived@@8 because ObjFileWriter::add_vtable unconditionally emits
RTTI structures keyed by class_name without deduplication.

* Fix use-after-move: defer vtable_info move until after secondary vtable registration

* Guard secondary vptr stores with Itanium ABI check to fix LNK2019 on COFF

The emitVptrStores lambda was emitting MemberStore ops referencing
$flash$vtable$ secondary vtable symbols on both ELF and COFF paths.
These symbols are only generated by the ELF vtable emission code,
causing LNK2019 unresolved externals on Windows/COFF.

* Fix COFF virtual base construction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify collectReachableVBases: avoid redundant traversal of virtual_bases and base_classes

Since StructTypeInfo::virtual_bases already contains all transitively
reachable virtual bases, we only need to iterate virtual_bases for
collection and recurse through non-virtual bases in base_classes.
Remove the redundant virtual-base collection from base_classes and
the unnecessary recursion into virtual bases (which are shared and
already fully captured by the virtual_bases list).

* Fix use-after-move: save ctor_decl_op.mangled_name before std::move

* Add TODO comment documenting missing destructor thunks in secondary vtables

* Add known issue: remove default ConstructorVariant parameters in NameMangling.h

commit.message:
feat: add eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect (#988)

Adds
[`eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect`](https://github.com/nickjvandyke/eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect)
to catch unnecessary `useEffect` usage — derived state, chained state
updates, event handlers in effects, and similar anti-patterns.

## Changes

flat config

```js
import reactYouMightNotNeedAnEffect from "eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect"

export default [
  // ...
  reactYouMightNotNeedAnEffect.configs.recommended,
  // ...
]
```

Enables 9 rules as warnings covering: `no-derived-state`,
`no-chain-state-updates`, `no-event-handler`, `no-initialize-state`,
`no-pass-data-to-parent`, `no-pass-live-state-to-parent`,
`no-adjust-state-on-prop-change`, `no-reset-all-state-on-prop-change`,
`no-empty-effect`.

commit.message:
fix: Enable testTagsAsResourceId for Maestro id: selectors (#308)

Adds testTagsAsResourceId = true to Scaffold's semantics modifier
to expose Compose testTags as resource IDs in the accessibility tree.
This allows Maestro flows to find elements using id: selectors
(e.g., id: 'nav_history', id: 'search_bar').

Without this, Maestro's id: selector cannot find testTag elements
because Compose does not expose testTags as resource-id by default.

Fixes #307

commit.message:
fix(build): prevent 0-byte static pages from I/O contention

- Reduce parallel write batch from 300 to 200 to lower I/O pressure
- Add sync repair pass after flush: detects and rewrites 0-byte files
- Log individual write failures with file paths (was only logging count)
- flatContentPlugin: skip copying 0-byte source files to flat paths

Root cause: writeFile uses O_TRUNC which creates 0-byte files before
writing content. Under heavy I/O contention (153K+ parallel writes),
some writes fail after truncation, leaving 0-byte files that Google
indexes as soft-404s.

commit.message:
feat: implement mempalace MCP server

Complete Rust implementation of the steel-memory memory palace MCP server.

## Architecture
- rmcp 1.3.0 with tool_router/tool_handler macros
- SQLite (rusqlite bundled) for vector and knowledge graph storage
- fastembed with ort-load-dynamic for local neural embeddings (no download at build time)
- tokio async runtime with spawn_blocking for SQLite ops

## Files added
- src/config.rs – palace path/DB config
- src/types.rs – Drawer, SearchResult, Entity, Triple structs
- src/error.rs – MemPalaceError type
- src/storage/vector.rs – VectorStorage with cosine similarity search
- src/storage/knowledge_graph.rs – KnowledgeGraph with triple management
- src/storage/palace_graph.rs – PalaceGraph BFS traversal & tunnel detection
- src/core/dialect.rs – AAAK compression (entities/emotions/flags)
- src/core/aaak_spec.rs – AAAK dialect specification constant
- src/core/layers.rs – MemoryStack (Layer 0 identity + Layer 1 AAAK)
- src/mcp/server.rs – MemPalace struct with 22 MCP tools
- src/lib.rs – library target for integration tests
- tests/integration_test.rs – unit-style tests (no network/model downloads)

## Tools exposed (22 total)
mempalace_status, list_wings, list_rooms, get_taxonomy,
search, check_duplicate, wake_up, recall,
build_graph, traverse_graph, find_tunnels, palace_graph_stats,
add_drawer, delete_drawer,
kg_query, kg_add, kg_invalidate, kg_timeline, kg_stats,
diary_write, diary_read, get_aaak_spec

commit.message:
feat(salary): replace estimates with USTAT 2024 official Ticino data

Replace rough salary estimates with official data from USTAT Ticino
(Rilevazione svizzera della struttura dei salari, cubi_RSS_02, 2024).

Key changes:
- TICINO_SECTOR_MEDIANS: all 16 sectors updated with USTAT p50 data
  by NOGA 2008 sector × position level (junior/mid/senior)
- Ticino discount corrected: was ×0.90 (10% below national), actual
  is ~0.82 (18% below) per USTAT 2024 (CHF 5,393 vs ~6,600 national)
- Min/max range widened: ×0.80/×1.25 (was ×0.85/×1.15) based on
  actual p25/p75 interquartile ratios from USTAT data
- salaryData.ts: all 15 sectors × 4 professions recalibrated to
  match USTAT Ticino figures (generally lower than previous estimates)
- Added NOGA code references per sector for traceability
- Source attribution: USTAT cubi_RSS_02 2024, BFS LSE, Salarium

Notable corrections (sectors most affected):
- Education: was overstated by 20-36% (Ticino education pays less)
- Engineering: junior/mid were 10-18% too high
- Hospitality: senior was overstated by ~29%
- Finance senior: was understated by ~44% (USTAT shows CHF 184k)
- Retail: was understated by 18-37% across all levels

commit.message:
Enforce foundation-first pattern in PE engineering plan

PE Agent now generates engineering plans with a mandatory T1 foundation
task that scaffolds the project structure, shared interfaces, base
classes, and DI registration before any parallel work begins.

Changes to PE planning prompt:
- T1 must always be Project Foundation & Scaffolding (High, no deps)
- All other tasks must depend on T1 for parallel safety
- Tasks must own distinct files (vertical slices, not horizontal layers)
- Star-topology dependencies: maximize tasks that depend only on T1
- FilePlan must list exact file ownership per task

Post-parse enforcement (EnsureFoundationFirstPattern):
- Reorders foundation task to first position if AI misplaced it
- Clears dependencies from T1, adds T1 dependency to all others
- Detects and logs file ownership overlaps between tasks
